behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` returns the correct total from cells A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: the failure boundary is range resolution -> cell fetch -> SUM function execution, which is a module handoff issue (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. TWO-PASS down-level check: Unit was considered, but behavior text explicitly requires `A1:A10` reference expansion in engine context, not only SUM arithmetic, so a pure function test is insufficient. Rejection gate for higher levels: behavior has no subprocess/HTTP/AI content, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with A1..A10 populated, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)` through the normal parser+resolver+function pipeline, and assert the computed value; include mixed numeric types to expose range-to-function handoff bugs.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing another formula cell computes the correct cascaded value.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: this failure appears when dependency resolution and evaluator outputs are handed across cells (`=B1+1`, `B1=A1*2`). Unit on one expression evaluator can miss stale/read-order bugs. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. TWO-PASS up-level check: System rejected because behavior text is purely in-memory formula cascade.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`, evaluate workbook, assert C1 matches expected and that B1 is evaluated before C1 within engine dependency logic.

behaviors.description: Circular references are detected and surfaced as circular-reference errors.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: cycle detection requires interaction between reference graph construction and evaluation traversal; a single-function unit test may not catch engine-level loop prevention behavior. REAL-INFRA: no external infra needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. TWO-PASS up-level check: System rejected since loop/error behavior is internal dependency-graph logic, not OS/process/network.
behaviors.test_description: Build a workbook with A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1`, run evaluation through normal engine entrypoint, assert a circular-reference error is returned and evaluation terminates without infinite loop.

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact-match returns the right value and missing key returns an error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: real failure boundary is lookup-range resolution plus VLOOKUP semantics (key search + not-found error mapping). Unit-only function tests can miss formula/range plumbing errors. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. TWO-PASS down-level check: Unit considered, but behavior text is formula-engine behavior over a lookup table range, not just isolated table-search logic.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a sheet range as lookup table, evaluate formulas using exact-match VLOOKUP for present and absent keys, assert returned value and specific not-found error code.

behaviors.description: Nested `IF` conditions evaluate to the correct branch result.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: behavior is expression semantics for nested conditionals; failure boundary is parser/evaluator logic within one local evaluation component. MOCK-MISS does not force module-boundary testing here. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. TWO-PASS up-level check rejected: behavior text does not require cross-module state propagation, only correct branch selection.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")` for representative A1 values in a local evaluator context and assert exact outputs.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins values with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Unit: this is function-level string assembly/empty-handling logic; the failure boundary is local function behavior. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. TWO-PASS up-level check rejected: behavior text focuses on output formatting rules, not dependency graph or persistence/module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Call formula evaluation for CONCATENATE cases with non-empty and empty operands, asserting separator placement and that empty cells are omitted rather than producing extra blanks.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (e.g., `Sheet2!A1`) resolves when target sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: failure boundary is workbook sheet registry -> reference resolver -> evaluator handoff. Unit tests on arithmetic/parser alone miss sheet-namespace wiring defects. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff. TWO-PASS up-level check rejected: behavior text is in-memory sheet resolution, not system infra.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 formula `=Sheet2!A1+1` and populated Sheet2!A1, evaluate via full engine path, assert resolved value and no reference error.

behaviors.description: When an input cell changes, dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: this is classic module interaction (dependency graph invalidation + scheduler/order + evaluator). Unit of one function cannot expose cross-node recomputation order bugs. Integration-brake: (a) no OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM orchestration. TWO-PASS up-level check: System considered for timing/order realism, rejected because behavior text requires logical topological correctness, not OS-level concurrency.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency chain/diamond formulas, mutate a source cell, trigger recalculation, assert final values and (if observable) evaluation order matches topological constraints.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores both formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: failure boundary spans edit model, undo/redo stack, and recalculation propagation; isolated unit tests miss state handoff regressions. Integration-brake: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent sequence, (d) no LLM orchestration. TWO-PASS up-level check rejected: behavior text is internal engine state restoration, not external system behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Set dependent formulas, edit one formula, compute changed results, invoke undo, then assert restored formula string and restored downstream computed values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save/load round-trip preserves formulas, values, and floating-point precision.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 Integration: this requires serializer/deserializer + workbook model + filesystem wiring; real failure is cross-module round-trip integrity. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem is sufficient and fits Integration per level definition; no subprocess/network needed. TWO-PASS up-level check: System considered, rejected because behavior text asks for save/load correctness, not OS-specific file events/locking/network failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with formulas and high-precision floats, save to temp file in custom binary format, reload through normal loader, and assert formula text equality plus numeric equality within strict precision tolerance.
